title: stipagrostis plumosa subsp. seminuda
intro: Stipagrostis plumosa subsp. seminuda, also known as the needle grass or grass of the sand, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a hardy and adaptable plant that thrives in dry and sandy areas, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Stipagrostis plumosa subsp. seminuda can be prepared as an infusion or decoction, and has been used to make herbal teas, tonics, and salves, to take advantage of its health benefits.
